title: Rationale block embedded in every IR document
description: Structured reasoning chain the LLM emits alongside the IR. Drives the runtime's chat-summary, collapsible sections, and downloadable audit document. Replaces bespoke rationale Python in the DraftsMan runtime.

chat_summary.description: 3–5 sentences shown inline in the chat thread. Tell the engineer: what you designed (1 sentence), the key decisions (1–2 sentences), any flags or assumptions (1 sentence), and an invitation to refine.

title.description: Section heading shown in the collapsible audit, e.g. 'Illumination', 'Layout', 'Circuits'

summary.description: Single-paragraph summary of this design dimension. If the section is not applicable to this design, set summary to explain why and leave decisions empty.

decisions.description: Discrete decisions made in this section. May be empty when the section's summary explains non-applicability.

rule.description: The deterministic rule or engineering principle invoked (e.g. 'BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 entry for open_plan_office')

code_clause.description: Standard clause reference (e.g. 'BS 7671 § 411.3.3', 'IEC 60364-4-43', 'IEC 61439-2:2020 Annex 101', 'AD Part L 2021 Section 5')

inputs.description: Structured map of the values that drove this decision

$validation_note: Hard-validation rules: chat_summary length is enforced; sections.minItems >= 1; Section.decisions MAY be empty as long as Section.summary is non-empty. Severity policy is set by the runtime — schema-failure is currently a hard fail with one retry via the validator prompt.
